body:
## What
Seven small surgical edits in \`dream-server/bin/dream-host-agent.py\` that harden the host agent's install and model-validation paths against silent failures, misleading error surfacing, and a couple of long-form Compose volume cases the pre-create logic was missing.

## Why
The host agent is the dispatcher between the dashboard-api and Docker. Most of its current failure paths either truncate diagnostics (head-truncation of stderr drops the actual error in favour of the layer-pull preamble), swallow OSError silently (frozen progress bars, no journal record), or conflate distinct error classes (HTTP 403 vs 500). When something goes wrong, operators see a misleading symptom and have to read the journal — often to find that nothing was logged because the failure was swallowed.

Concretely:

1. **Narrow \`docker compose pull\`.** Currently runs with the FULL merged compose stack, so a single \`\${VAR:?}\` guard in any unrelated extension's compose aborts the pull for the target extension with an error message naming the wrong extension.
2. **\`stderr[:N]\` head-truncation at three sites in \`_handle_install\`.** Docker-compose puts the real error at the END of stderr — head-truncation drops exactly the diagnostic bytes the operator needs.
3. **\`_write_model_status\` silent OSError swallow.** Disk-full or permission errors freeze the dashboard progress bar with no journal trace.
4. **\`_recreate_llama_server\` silent docker-run failure.** Logs but does not raise; caller waits the full 5-minute health-check timeout before rolling back. Also reachable on Linux native via the \`_compose_restart_llama_server\` no-\`.compose-flags\` fallback (not just Windows/in-container).
5. **HTTP 403/500 conflation in model-library validation.** A corrupt or missing \`config/model-library.json\` returns the same 403 as a genuinely-denied model. The same silent-except exists in the parallel \`_handle_model_list\` handler.
6. **\`_precreate_data_dirs\` misses long-form Compose volumes.** \`str(vol).split(":")[0]\` works for the string short-form \`./data/foo:/target\` but stringifies the dict long-form \`{type: bind, source: ./foo, target: /bar}\` into a Python repr and misses the bind source. Extensions using long-form volumes hit the "Docker auto-creates as root:root" failure mode.
7. **\`_precreate_data_dirs\` doesn't skip non-pre-expanded sources.** \`~\`, \`\$VAR\`, backtick, backslash get fed to the relative-bind logic and produce literal garbage directories on disk.

## How
Single file: \`dream-server/bin/dream-host-agent.py\`.

1. \`_handle_install._run_install\` derives a narrow \`pull_flags\` list by filtering out \`-f\` entries pointing at OTHER extensions' compose fragments under \`EXTENSIONS_DIR\` / \`USER_EXTENSIONS_DIR\` via an inline \`_is_other_ext_compose\` helper. The pull step uses \`pull_flags\`; the \`up\` step keeps full \`flags\` so cross-service \`depends_on\` still resolves. Conservative: when a path can't be resolved it stays in the list (better to over-pull than to silently drop a needed file).
2. Three \`stderr[:N]\` truncations flipped to \`[-N:]\` (lines 1133, 1145, 1157).
3. \`_write_model_status\`: \`except OSError: pass\` -> \`except OSError as e: logger.warning(...)\`. Behavior preserved (does not raise) so the activate flow continues, but the failure now lands in the journal.
4. \`_recreate_llama_server\`: \`raise RuntimeError(f"docker run failed: {result.stderr[-500:]}")\` after the existing \`logger.error\`. The outer try at the activate-flow level catches and surfaces a real error within seconds instead of the 5-minute hang. This single change covers both call sites — the in-container path (Windows/WSL2) and the no-\`.compose-flags\` fallback path (Linux native).
5. \`_handle_model_download\` adds a \`catalog_ok\` sentinel and returns HTTP 500 (\`"Model catalog unavailable"\`) when the library file is missing or unreadable. HTTP 403 (\`"Model not in library catalog"\`) is now reserved strictly for the genuine policy-denial path. The same \`logger.exception\` + 500 pattern is also applied to \`_handle_model_list\`'s parallel silent except.
6 + 7. \`_precreate_data_dirs\` handles dict-form Compose volumes (\`type: bind\` only; extracts \`source\`) and skips sources starting with \`~\`, \`\$\`, backtick, or backslash before the existing relative-bind filter.

## Testing
- \`make lint\` PASS (shell syntax + Python compile).
- \`make test\` PASS.
- \`python3 -m py_compile\` + AST parse PASS.
- \`pytest --collect-only\` for dashboard-api collects all 695 tests cleanly (\`test_hooks.py\` imports the host-agent module — no symbol regressions).
- Static spot-check of all 7 edits: line numbers, function bodies, edit shapes match intent.
- Live end-to-end on macOS Apple Silicon: \`dream stop\` then \`install.sh --non-interactive\` against an existing install. Exit 0, all 6 phases complete, all 11 core services come up healthy. Host process verified running new code (\`ps -ef | grep host-agent\` confirms the running PID reads \`/Volumes/X/dreamserver/bin/dream-host-agent.py\`; grep on that file confirms \`_is_other_ext_compose\`, \`catalog_ok\`, \`stderr[-500:]\` patterns are all present).

## Review
Local CG approved with two PR-body documentation requirements, both addressed below.

## Known considerations
**Behavior change.** \`_handle_model_download\` previously returned HTTP 403 "Model not in library catalog" for both genuine policy denials AND for missing/unreadable catalog files. Clients that relied on 403 to detect "no catalog yet" must update — they will now see HTTP 500 with \`"Model catalog unavailable"\` in that case. The 403 remains for the legitimate "model exists in catalog but not allowed" path.

**Asymmetry between \`_handle_model_list\` and \`_handle_model_download\` on missing catalog.** \`_handle_model_list\` returns HTTP 200 + empty library (browse semantics — a fresh install legitimately has no catalog yet, and the dashboard splash should still render). \`_handle_model_download\` returns HTTP 500 (validate-this-specific-model semantics — catalog absence breaks the security check; lying with 403 would conflate a broken install with a policy denial). Inline comment in the diff explains the choice.

**\`_is_other_ext_compose\` is an inline closure** capturing \`service_id\` and \`ext_roots\` from \`_run_install\`. Hoisting to module level would buy nothing material at this scale — kept inline for readability.

## Platform impact
- **macOS** (native llama-server, Docker for the rest): All edits active except \`_recreate_llama_server\` itself (Apple Silicon uses \`_launch_native_llama_server\`); the raise still applies if the in-container path is ever triggered. Manual reinstall verified.
- **Linux Docker**: All edits active. \`_recreate_llama_server\` raise covers the previously-undocumented Linux native fallback path (when \`.compose-flags\` is missing) — operator sees a real error within seconds instead of the 5-minute hang.
- **Windows/WSL2**: All edits active. \`_recreate_llama_server\` is the primary activate path here and was the most user-visible offender for the silent-fail bug.
title: fix(host-agent): runtime hygiene — narrow pull, surface failures, normalize bind volumes
